problem: The number $5\,41G\,507\,2H6$ is divisible by $72.$ If $G$ and $H$ each represent a single digit, what is the sum of all distinct possible values of the product $GH?$ (Count each possible value of $GH$ only once, even if it results from multiple $G,$ $H$ pairs.)

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we need to check divisibility by $8$ and $9$ for the given number $5\,41G\,507\,2H6$ to be divisible by $72$?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we determine if the last three digits, denoted by $2H6$, are divisible by $8$?

classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.label: r
